page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DLSv5 server family. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this server features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It operates on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This configuration does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96ls_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities. The server achieved a strong multi-core CPU performance score of 93,251.87 in stress-ng and a Passmark CPU score of 76,315.59. Cryptographic execution is highly efficient, with the Passmark CPU encryption test reaching a top-tier rating of 87,240.76, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and VAES. Memory subsystem performance is also robust, recording uncached read and write bandwidths of 329.31 GB/sec and 140.95 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded decompression and software compilation tasks also score in the top 25% tier.

The primary tradeoff of the D96ls_v5 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio and the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high compute density, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for parallel processing tasks, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, cryptographic operations, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) operating at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM. This configuration lacks local storage and GPUs, prioritizing raw CPU density. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 93,251.87 and top-tier cryptographic execution. The server's 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications, but delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive tasks. It is highly suited for multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, cryptographic workloads, and parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 192 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized VM with 96 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, and 192 GB RAM, designed for high-density compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six vCPUs and robust multi-core performance for parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong multi-core CPU and top-tier cryptographic benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Optimized for multi-threaded compilation, web serving, and parallel computing
